page: Microsoft Azure DC96ads_cc_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the DCADCCV5 family. It is powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server type includes 3686 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. The local disk capacity provides substantial space for temporary data, while the Premium Storage capability allows for high-throughput persistent disk attachments. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the DC96ads_cc_v5 offers a high density of dedicated compute cores and local storage, making it suitable for large-scale deployments that require balanced resource allocation without the overhead of a GPU. However, the lack of hardware accelerators like GPUs limits its utility for deep learning workloads. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and large local storage capacity make this instance appropriate for general-purpose enterprise workloads, relational databases, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure DC96ads_cc_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance from the DCADCCV5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated AMD vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. The instance is equipped with 3686 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations, though it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Lacking dedicated GPU hardware, this server is designed for non-accelerated workloads. The combination of high-density dedicated compute resources and substantial local storage makes this instance suitable for large-scale general-purpose applications, database hosting, and enterprise-level multi-threaded processing.
